state_description: Reduced-form biological removal state representing land-sector sequestration / carbon-forestry style removals with strong land-use and permanence caveats.
cost_components_summary: Representative full removal supply cost, including MRV and permanence/handling proxies. Commodity inputs listed separately where relevant. Policy transfer payments excluded.
input_basis_notes: Direct operational energy inputs only. For land sequestration most cost is implicit land, establishment and MRV cost rather than explicit commodity use.
emissions_boundary_notes: Negative process-emissions coefficient denotes net removal. Positive energy-emissions coefficient captures on-site operational emissions where present.
rollout_limit_notes: Activity cap is an indicative annual upper bound only; land competition, permanence and infrastructure constraints are not fully modelled.
availability_conditions: Land sequestration is available immediately but constrained; DACCS is exploratory and effectively unavailable in material quantities before 2030.
evidence_summary: Removals are treated cautiously, using official Australian projections for land-sink context and international DAC cost evidence for engineered removals.
derivation_method: Exploratory supply-state synthesis. Kept separate from ordinary production sectors so users can enable or disable removals explicitly.
review_notes: Retained as an exploratory land-removal supply state, not as a complete land-use model.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Land-use competition, permanence, reversal risk and MRV modules for biological sinks; explicit DAC process chain and storage network for DACCS.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Would likely map to separate negative-emissions technologies in TIMES with explicit storage links and policy controls.
